name: Integra Funding Solutions
description_short: Bank-backed invoice factoring and working capital solutions for businesses needing same-day funding. Division of T Bank, N.A. with transparent pricing and no hidden fees.
description_long:
Integra Funding Solutions operates as a division of T Bank, N.A., a nationally chartered bank and FDIC member. The company specializes in invoice factoring and working capital solutions designed to provide rapid cash flow to businesses that invoice their customers. Founded on principles of transparency and direct customer service, Integra positions itself as an alternative to independent factoring companies by leveraging its parent bank's stability and regulatory oversight.

Integra's core offering is invoice factoring—the purchase of unpaid invoices at a discount to provide immediate cash. They emphasize same-day funding for invoices submitted by 1 PM CT and serve specialized industries including freight and transportation, oil and gas, staffing, aerospace, and manufacturing. The company offers industry-specific solutions such as non-recourse freight factoring with fuel card discounts, flexible working capital for oil and gas service companies, and weekly payroll funding for staffing agencies. All funding operates through a transparent, all-inclusive rate structure with no additional fees for wire transfers, document uploads, or background checks.

Integra's primary distinguishing factors are its bank-backed status, direct human customer service (no call centers or automated systems), dedicated account managers assigned to each client, in-house credit decisions made in the Fort Worth office, and FDIC regulation through its parent bank. The company holds IFA certification as a member of the International Factoring Association, maintains A+ accreditation with the Better Business Bureau, and reports five-star ratings on Google customer reviews. They explicitly position themselves against independent factors by highlighting regulatory stability, sustainable pricing without hidden fees, and long-term partnership reliability.

Investors and business owners should understand that Integra is a legitimate, bank-backed factoring provider rather than a traditional lender. Invoice factoring is asset-based financing (not debt) and does not appear as new debt on balance sheets. However, factoring costs money—the company charges an all-inclusive rate on the invoice amount discounted—and is appropriate only for businesses with consistent invoice-based revenue. The service is not a loan alternative for general business expenses but specifically for converting accounts receivable into immediate working capital. Businesses should verify that their customer base will accept the Notice of Assignment required by factoring arrangements.
pros: Same-day funding available for invoices submitted by 1 PM CT with documented 24-hour turnaround capability
Transparent, all-inclusive rate structure with explicitly no hidden fees, junk charges, wire transfer fees, or document upload costs
FDIC-regulated parent bank (T Bank, N.A.) provides security and stability unavailable with independent private equity-backed factors
Dedicated account manager assigned from day one with direct phone line to Fort Worth office—no call centers, ticket systems, or automated menus
In-house credit team in Fort Worth office making invoice approval decisions in hours rather than days
Specialized industry solutions for freight/transportation (non-recourse options, fuel cards), oil and gas (long contract terms), and staffing (weekly payroll funding)
IFA certification, BBB A+ accreditation, and five-star Google reviews from verified customers indicating consistent customer satisfaction
cons: Factoring is not a loan and requires ongoing monthly costs tied to invoice volume—more expensive than a traditional line of credit for businesses with strong credit
Requires customers to receive Notice of Assignment, which may concern some clients or affect customer relationships if not handled proactively
Only processes invoices submitted by 1 PM CT for same-day funding—invoices submitted after cutoff experience delayed funding
Limited to invoice-based business models; not suitable for retailers, e-commerce, or service businesses without formal invoicing to corporate/commercial clients
Website does not disclose specific discount rates or fee structures, requiring direct contact for pricing—makes cost comparison difficult upfront
best_for: Freight and transportation companies (especially owner-operators and carriers) needing fuel cards and non-recourse factoring options
Staffing agencies with weekly payroll obligations and 30-day client billing cycles requiring bridge funding
Oil and gas service companies managing long contract terms, equipment costs, and seasonal cash flow volatility
Growing B2B service businesses (aerospace, manufacturing, consulting) with consistent corporate clients and invoice-based revenue
diagnosis: Integra Funding Solutions is best for established B2B businesses with consistent corporate invoicing that need rapid working capital conversion and value stable, transparent service over the cheapest rates. The main caveat is that invoice factoring is a permanent operational cost (not a one-time loan), requires customers to accept assignment notices, and is only viable for businesses with recurring invoice-based revenue—not a solution for retailers, startups without established customer bases, or businesses seeking traditional debt financing.
services: Invoice factoring with same-day funding (24 hours if submitted by 1 PM CT)
Working capital solutions for businesses with long customer payment terms
Freight factoring with fuel card discounts and non-recourse options for carriers
Oil and gas working capital financing for service companies
Weekly payroll funding for staffing agencies
Dedicated account manager assignment and ongoing account management
Secure online portal for invoice submission and account management
In-house credit review and invoice approval decisions
Transparent rate comparison and free rate quotes
Flexible contract terms with fair termination policies
Industry-specialized funding structured to match operational cycles
FDIC-backed capital and regulatory oversight through T Bank, N.A.
